📦 Convert Data Frame to List by Grouping

Source: R/df2list.R
df2list.Rd

Group a data frame by one column and aggregate values from another column into lists. Useful for creating marker gene sets: e.g., cell_type -> marker_genes.

Usage

df2list(data, key_col, value_col, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

data

A data.frame or tibble to be grouped.

key_col

Character. Name of the column to group by (e.g., "cell_type").

value_col

Character. Name of the column to aggregate into lists (e.g., "marker_genes").

verbose

Logical. Whether to show progress messages. Default is TRUE.

Value

A tibble with one row per key and a list-column of aggregated values.

Examples

df <- data.frame(
  cell_type = c(rep("B_CELLS_MEMORY", 5), rep("T_CELLS_CD8", 4), rep("MONOCYTES", 3)),
  marker_genes = c("AIM2", "BANK1", "CD19", "CD27", "CD37",
                   "CD8A", "CD8B", "GZMB", "PRF1",
                   "LYZ", "S100A9", "CD14")
)
df2list(df, key_col = "cell_type", value_col = "marker_genes")
#> ✔ 🎉 Successfully grouped 3 rows by `cell_type` and aggregated `marker_genes` as lists.
#> # A tibble: 3 × 2
#>   cell_type      marker_genes
#>   <chr>          <list>      
#> 1 B_CELLS_MEMORY <chr [5]>   
#> 2 MONOCYTES      <chr [3]>   
#> 3 T_CELLS_CD8    <chr [4]>
